name: Femasys — OBGYN Office-Based Fertility (FemaSeed)
version_note: <<RAJIV: PASTE CLIENT DISCLAIMER HERE BEFORE RUNNING>>
description: Office-based OBGYN / women's health practices doing any fertility work — IUI, medicated cycles, infertility evaluation/testing, or fertility consultation — who are candidates to adopt FemaSeed (intratubal insemination) as an upgrade to in-office IUI. The v9 net is intentionally wide: any fertility activity qualifies for fit and nothing is a hard gate. Screening is carried by exclusions instead — IVF/REI fertility centers and hospital / health-system-affiliated practices are removed. FemVue practices are existing Femasys relationships and the strongest warm leads.
scoring_notes: v9 (client-confirmed). Design: fit is soft (no required_for_bullseye gate) and widened to any fertility activity; the screening burden moves onto exclusions, which are load-bearing. FIT signals (IUI, medicated cycles, infertility eval incl. HSG, fertility consultation, cycle-monitoring ultrasound, FemVue) feed fit only and use strict phrase-binding so generic OBGYN copy does not false-positive. Core fertility signals (IUI, medicated cycles, infertility eval) carry the heaviest weight so a genuinely strong fertility practice reaches Bullseye on its own merits. FemVue is a warm-lead accelerator (boost + floor_tier Contender), deliberately NOT large enough to be required for Bullseye. READINESS (cash-pay / patient financing, reinforced by elective-aesthetic cash-pay) reflects adoption velocity. EXCLUSIONS: IVF/REI fertility center (exclude_if_yes) and hospital_owned / health_system_affiliated (structural gates) drop out-of-profile practices. bullseye_min_score is 80 (set in run_config): the score blend caps near 96 for a perfect record, so 80 leaves Bullseye reachable for strong fertility fit with or without FemVue, rather than requiring near-perfection.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly offer IUI as a service? Answer yes ONLY when the page contains the exact term 'IUI' or the full phrase 'intrauterine insemination'. Do NOT infer from generic words like 'fertility', 'consultation', 'treatment', or 'family planning' — the specific term must appear verbatim.
signals.note: Core fit, heaviest fertility signal. Direct Femasys procedure match: an in-office IUI service line is the clearest signal a practice already does the office-based fertility work FemaSeed upgrades. Phrase-bound so it does not fire on generic fertility copy.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice offer medicated or stimulated ovulation cycles? Answer yes ONLY when one of these exact phrases appears: 'medicated cycle', 'medicated cycles', 'stimulated cycle', or 'ovulation induction'. Do NOT match bare words like 'cycle', 'medication', or 'treatment'.
signals.note: Strong fit. Medicated-cycle management indicates an active fertility-treatment workflow, the same patient population FemaSeed serves. Phrase-bound to avoid false positives on routine cycle/medication language.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice offer infertility evaluation or testing? Answer yes ONLY when one of these exact phrases appears: 'infertility evaluation', 'infertility workup', 'infertility diagnosis', 'fertility testing', 'infertility testing', 'HSG', or 'hysterosalpingogram'. Do NOT match bare standalone words such as 'evaluation', 'testing', 'diagnosis', or 'consultation' — those false-positive on nearly every OBGYN site; the full fertility-bound phrase must be present.
signals.note: Moderate fit. Office-based infertility workup (HSG folds in here, not as a separate high-value flag) signals diagnostic fertility activity short of full ART. Strict phrase-binding keeps generic 'evaluation/testing' copy out.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice offer an infertility or fertility consultation? Answer yes ONLY when the exact phrase 'infertility consultation' or 'fertility consultation' appears. Do NOT match the bare word 'consultation' on its own.
signals.note: Weak fit. A named fertility consultation line shows fertility intent but is the lowest-commitment fertility signal, so it carries the smallest fit weight. Phrase-bound to exclude generic 'consultation' offers.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice perform fertility cycle monitoring by ultrasound? Answer yes ONLY when one of these exact phrases appears: 'follicle monitoring', 'cycle monitoring', 'monitoring ultrasound', or 'transvaginal ultrasound'. Do NOT match the bare word 'ultrasound' or 'monitoring' alone.
signals.note: A plus, not a gate. Cycle-monitoring ultrasound reinforces that fertility treatment happens in-office and adds confidence, but its absence is not disqualifying, so it carries a modest fit weight and no penalty when not found.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice mention FemVue anywhere on the site? Answer yes ONLY when the exact product name 'FemVue' appears.
signals.note: Warm-lead accelerator, not a gate. FemVue is Femasys's own product; a practice already using it is an existing relationship and the lowest-friction FemaSeed adoption path. It carries a meaningful fit boost plus floor_tier (a confirmed FemVue practice never falls below Contender), but it is deliberately NOT large enough to be required for Bullseye: strong fertility fit reaches Bullseye on its own merits, and FemVue accelerates rather than gatekeeps.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website show explicit evidence of cash-pay / self-pay capability or third-party patient financing? Answer yes when any of these is present: 'CareCredit', 'FSA', 'HSA', 'patient financing', published self-pay or package pricing for fertility or related services, the phrase 'out-of-network', a statement that a service is not covered by insurance, or offering superbills/invoices for reimbursement. Answer yes only when such language is explicitly present on the site.
signals.note: Readiness target. Direct-billing / financing infrastructure predicts adoption velocity for a patient-pay device workflow. Reinforced by the elective-aesthetic signal (S-ICP-008) when explicit cash-pay language is absent but elective cash-pay services are present.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice offer elective, aesthetic, or cosmetic services that are typically elective, non-covered, or patient-pay in routine commercial practice? Answer yes when one or more of these is explicitly named: a med spa / MedSpa / aesthetic center; aesthetics or esthetics; vaginal rejuvenation or energy-based intimate devices (for example Votiva, ThermiVa, MonaLisa Touch, FemiLift, diVa, Emsella); aesthetic gynecology or cosmetic genital surgery (labiaplasty, vaginoplasty, labial reduction); injectable aesthetics (Botox, Dysport, dermal fillers); body contouring (CoolSculpting, Emsculpt, truSculpt); energy-based skin devices or laser hair removal; IV therapy or IV drip; or weight-loss injectables (GLP-1, semaglutide, tirzepatide). Answer yes only when one or more such services are explicitly named on the site.
signals.note: Readiness reinforcer. Elective aesthetic / cash-pay services are patient-pay by definition, so their presence implies direct-billing infrastructure even when no explicit cash-pay language appears. Weight 0: it contributes only by inferring the cash-pay readiness target (S-ICP-007), never independently inflating fit.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice operate as, or hold itself out as, an IVF or reproductive-endocrinology fertility center? Answer yes when any of these appears: 'IVF', 'in vitro fertilization', 'egg retrieval', 'embryo transfer', 'reproductive endocrinology', 'reproductive endocrinologist', 'REI', or the practice naming itself a 'fertility center'.
signals.note: Exclusion. A confirmed yes means a specialized fertility center that routes patients to in-house ART and male-factor pathways, not the office-based OBGYN profile FemaSeed upgrades. The NPI taxonomy structural gate (rei_on_staff) pre-filters most of these before crawl; this signal catches any that reach the LLM pass.
